info.name: Clubbucket Backend
info.description: Ready-to-use API collection for the Clubbucket backend. Run the backend, apply migrations, run `npm run db:seed`, then run Setup > Health, Auth > Login - Super Admin, Auth > Login - League Admin, and the portal login requests as needed. Login and create requests save tokens and IDs automatically. Includes League Admin CMS app config/categories/items, direct logo/media URL examples, and unauthenticated public mobile fan app read APIs, and League Admin white-label branding bootstrap APIs.

item.name: Setup
item.description: Use these first to confirm the backend is running.

request.description: Checks whether the Express app is running.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Shared authentication APIs for all portals. Login stores tokens automatically.

request.description: Logs in the seeded platform admin. Run `docker compose exec -T app npm run db:seed` first if this returns invalid_credentials.
Logs in the seeded LigaD1 league admin created by `npm run db:seed`. Saves organizationAccessToken and organizationRefreshToken.

exec: pm.test('league admin login returns 200', function () { pm.response.to.have.status(200); });

request.description: Logs in seeded player Oscar Lima created by npm run db:seed. Saves playerPortalAccessToken, playerPortalRefreshToken, and playerPortalPlayerId.

request.description: Rotates the current refresh token. The old refreshToken becomes invalid. This request saves the new tokens.
Revokes the current refresh token.
Creates a development reset token. In non-production, resetToken is returned and saved automatically.
Uses the latest resetToken. After this succeeds, update adminPassword to match newPassword before logging in again.
Changes the currently authenticated user's password. User identity comes from the Bearer accessToken.
Accepts an invitation token created by future internal-user APIs. Put the raw invite token in inviteToken first.

item.name: Player Portal
item.description: Player portal APIs. Run Auth > Login - Player Portal first, then Current Terms and Accept Terms before self-service routes. X-Player-Id is optional for seeded single-player users and useful for local/test multi-player disambiguation.

request.description: Returns the authenticated Player Portal context: user, membership, role, permissions, organization, player, active roster entry, squad, season/division, and live terms acceptance status.

request.description: Accepts the current Player Portal terms version for the authenticated player. Repeating the same version is idempotent.
